Acne.rationale: Salicylic acid (BHA) unclogs pores; benzoyl peroxide kills acne bacteria; adapalene/tretinoin normalises cell turnover; niacinamide reduces sebum and inflammation; azelaic acid is antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory; sulfur dries active lesions; mandelic acid exfoliates without the irritation glycolic can cause.

Hyperpigmentation.rationale: Vitamin C inhibits tyrosinase (the enzyme that produces melanin); niacinamide blocks melanosome transfer; alpha arbutin and kojic acid are tyrosinase inhibitors; tretinoin accelerates cell renewal to shed pigmented cells; tranexamic acid blocks plasmin-mediated melanogenesis (strong RCT evidence for melasma); mandelic acid lifts surface pigment gently.

Pores.rationale: Salicylic acid dissolves the sebum and debris that stretches pores; niacinamide tightens pore walls and regulates oil production; AHAs (glycolic, mandelic) and PHAs exfoliate the surface to reduce their appearance.

Fine Lines.rationale: Tretinoin and retinol have gold-standard RCT evidence for stimulating collagen and reducing wrinkle depth; bakuchiol is a plant-derived alternative with smaller but supportive RCTs and better tolerance; peptides and copper peptides signal fibroblasts to produce collagen; vitamin C is a cofactor in collagen synthesis; hyaluronic acid provides immediate plumping hydration.

Texture.rationale: Chemical exfoliation with AHAs (glycolic, lactic, mandelic) and BHA (salicylic) smooths rough surface texture by dissolving the bonds between dead skin cells; PHAs offer a gentler exfoliation suitable for sensitive skin; urea is keratolytic at higher concentrations; tretinoin normalises keratinisation for long-term refinement.

Dryness.rationale: Hyaluronic acid attracts and holds water in the skin; ceramides seal the lipid barrier to prevent trans-epidermal water loss; squalane mimics the skin's own sebum to reinforce the barrier; urea is a natural moisturising factor (NMF) component; panthenol and allantoin soothe and accelerate barrier repair.

Oiliness.rationale: Niacinamide suppresses sebum production; salicylic acid penetrates the follicle to clear excess oil; azelaic acid normalises keratinisation and reduces abnormal sebum output; sulfur and mandelic acid help degrease without stripping the barrier.

Sensitivity.rationale: Niacinamide calms redness and reinforces the barrier; ceramides restore disrupted lipid layers; centella asiatica (cica) reduces inflammation via asiaticoside; panthenol and allantoin soothe; beta-glucan and snail mucin support repair; azelaic acid is anti-inflammatory and well-tolerated.

Rosacea.rationale: Azelaic acid is FDA-approved for papulopustular rosacea (15% formulation has strong RCT evidence); niacinamide reduces flushing and reinforces a notoriously fragile barrier; sulfur is a classic anti-rosacea active; centella, ceramides, and panthenol calm inflammation and restore the barrier.

Sun Damage.rationale: Daily zinc-oxide (or chemical) sunscreen is the only intervention that prevents further damage; vitamin C neutralises UV-induced free radicals; tranexamic acid and niacinamide fade sun-induced pigmentation; retinoids reverse photoaging by stimulating collagen and accelerating turnover.

Eczema-prone.rationale: Eczema-prone barriers leak water and react to irritants. Ceramides restore the lipid matrix; panthenol, allantoin, and centella reduce inflammation; beta-glucan and snail mucin support repair; urea (low %) and hyaluronic acid hydrate; squalane is a gentle occlusive.
